From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Colombian footballer (1968–2021)

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Berrío* and the second or maternal family name is  *Gómez*.

**Luis Guillermo Berrío Gómez** (11 April 1968 – 11 April 2021) was a Colombian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") player and coach.

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guillermo_Berr%C3%ADo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

Born in [Amagá](/wiki/Amag%C3%A1 "Amagá"),[[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1) Berrío began his career in 1988, playing for [Envigado](/wiki/Envigado_F.C. "Envigado F.C."), [Atlético Huila](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Huila "Atlético Huila"), [Deportivo Pereira](/wiki/Deportivo_Pereira "Deportivo Pereira") and [Deportes Quindío](/wiki/Deportes_Quind%C3%ADo "Deportes Quindío"), and winning four promotions throughout his career.[[2]](#cite_note-auto-2) He also played in Peru for [Unión Minas](/wiki/Uni%C3%B3n_Minas "Unión Minas") and [Juan Aurich](/wiki/Juan_Aurich "Juan Aurich").[[2]](#cite_note-auto-2) He retired in 2004, whilst a Deportivo Pereira player.[[2]](#cite_note-auto-2)

## Coaching career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guillermo_Berr%C3%ADo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Coaching career")]

Berrío coached more than 300 games, for clubs including [Atlético Huila](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Huila "Atlético Huila"), [Independiente Medellín](/wiki/Independiente_Medell%C3%ADn "Independiente Medellín"), [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera_F.C. "Alianza Petrolera F.C.") and [Deportivo Pasto](/wiki/Deportivo_Pasto "Deportivo Pasto").[[1]](#cite_note-auto1-1)[[2]](#cite_note-auto-2)

## Later life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Guillermo_Berr%C3%ADo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Later life and death")]

Berrío died of a heart attack on his 53rd birthday.[[2]](#cite_note-auto-2)
